# Pittman's Enterprises Limited (Pittmans)

> Second-generation architectural, heavy civil, marine, and electrical contractors, building across Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada since 1978. 300+ completed projects. Turnkey, foundation to finish.

Head office: St. Paul's, NL (Gros Morne National Park area). Operating anywhere in Newfoundland & Labrador.
Phone: 709-243-2666 · Email: info@pittmans.ca

## What we do
- Architectural general contracting: performing arts centres, schools, arenas, public buildings (steel, wood, masonry; Behlen pre-engineered steel buildings)
- Heavy civil and earthworks: excavation, roads, site development, structural fills, underground services
- Marine infrastructure: wharves, dredging, marine concrete; specialty in armour stone breakwaters and coastal protection
- Electrical: licensed commercial and industrial contracting, in-house
- Blasting and crushing: licensed drill/blast, mobile crushing and screening, aggregate supply with placement
- Mining and remote sites: camps, pads, access development anywhere; company float capacity to 75 tonnes

## Capability
Typical project size: $500,000 to $20 million and up. Self-performs civil, structural, architectural, and electrical work. Fleet of 100+ units, from mini excavators to 50-tonne class machines, heavy haul floats, crushers, and drills. Sectors served: mining, energy, healthcare, education, municipal, and federal.

## Public contract record (verifiable)
Repeat federal contractor with two dozen awards on public record since 2012, competitively won. Representative awards across work types:
- Harbour works, Raleigh, NL (2022): $4.9 million, Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Largest federal award on record.
- Coastal erosion protection, Gros Morne National Park (2016): $2.4 million, Public Services and Procurement Canada
- Marine infrastructure (2023): $2.7 million, Fisheries and Oceans Canada
- Marine infrastructure (2025): $2.7 million, Fisheries and Oceans Canada
- Building and engineering services (2017): $708,000, Parks Canada
- Office building construction, multi-year (2021-2023): $1.3 million, Public Services and Procurement Canada
- Remote site preparation, weather station (2017): Environment and Climate Change Canada
Verify at search.open.canada.ca under vendor names "Pittman's Enterprises Limited" and "Pittman Enterprises Ltd." Provincial awards appear in Contract Awards Reports at gov.nl.ca/ppa. The federal record skews marine; institutional building work is contracted provincially, municipally, and privately and is documented under Notable projects below.

## Technology
GPS machine control; robotic total station; autonomous marine survey vessel; underwater ROV; drone photogrammetry; 3D and dynamic structural modelling.

## Notable projects
- Nurse Myra Bennett Centre for the Performing Arts, Cow Head (2021): 2,000 m2, 178-seat theatre, a $10-million-class facility delivered for Theatre Newfoundland Labrador, built from scratch
- Seven Corners Lookout, Gros Morne National Park (2025): sculptural timber viewing structures
- School, Port Hope Simpson, Labrador (2020)
- DND granulars contract, Cartwright, Labrador (2020): drilling, blasting, crushing
- Complete Care Pharmasave, Rocky Harbour (2016)
- NL Hydro tailrace access, Grand Falls (2013): steel truss structure
- Torrent River Salmon Interpretation Centre, Hawke's Bay (2005)
- CFLCO wash bay, Churchill Falls, Labrador (2004): Behlen steel building
- Harbour development, St. Paul's (2004): wharf and marine works
- Harbour works, Raleigh (2022): $4.9 million federal marine contract
- Coastal erosion protection, Gros Morne National Park (2016): $2.4 million federal contract
- Burgeo Academy school, Burgeo (2003)
- Mona's Place, Lodge Bay, Labrador (2002): Behlen frameless steel building
- Seniors housing, Cartwright, Labrador (1990)
- Wharf construction at Pinsent's Arm, Labrador (current)
- Mining exploration camp and pad construction, coastal Labrador (2026)
- Municipal water, sewer and road infrastructure across the province

## Credentials
COR safety certified. Member of the Newfoundland and Labrador Construction Association (NLCA), the Heavy Civil Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (HCANL), and the Merit Contractors Association of Newfoundland and Labrador. Bonded and insured through Canada's largest surety, with bonding capacity in excess of $50 million.
